represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n66\t//\n67\t// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n68\t// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n84\t\t//\n85\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n86\t\t// expected Go type, including:\n87\t\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n88\t\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n89\t\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n90\t\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n91\t\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n92\t\n93\t\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n94\t\t//\n95\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n96\t\t// including:\n97\t\t// - Value out of allowed range\n98\t\t// - String length violations\n99\t\t// - Pattern matching failures\n100\t\t// - Required field violations\n101\t\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n102\t\n103\t\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n104\t\t//\n105\t\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n106\t\t// including:\n107\t\t// - File not found\n108\t\t// - Permission denied\n109\t\t// - Read/write failures\n110\t\t// - File system errors\n111\t\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n112\t\n113\t\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n114\t\t//\n115\t\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n116\t\t// including:\n117\t\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n118\t\t// - Invalid nesting\n119\t\t// - Circular references\n120\t\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n121\t\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n122\t\n123\t\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n124\t\t//\n125\t\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n126\t\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n127\t\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n128\t\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n129\t\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n130\t\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n131\t)\n132\t\n133\t// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\n134\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n135\t\treturn string(v)\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\n139\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n140\t\tswitch v {\n141\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n142\t\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// File error codes\n166\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n167\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n168\t\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n169\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n170\t\n171\t\t// Parse error codes\n172\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n173\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n174\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n175\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n176\t\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n177\t\n178\t\t// Validation error codes\n179\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n180\t\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n181\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n182\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n183\t\n184\t\t// Schema error codes\n185\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n186\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n187\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n188\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n189\t)\n190\t\n191\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n192\t//\n193\t// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n194\t// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\n195\ttype ParseError struct {\n196\t\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n197\t\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n198\t\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n199\t\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n200\t\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n201\t\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n202\t\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n203\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n204\t\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n205\t\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n206\t}\n207\t\n208\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n209\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n210\t\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n211\t\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n212\t\t}\n213\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n214\t}\n215\t\n216\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n217\tfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n218\t\treturn pe.ErrorType\n219\t}\n220\t\n221\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n222\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n223\t\treturn pe.ContextStr\n224\t}\n225\t\n226\t// Error implements the error interface.\n227\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n228\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n229\t\n230\t\t// Build base error with location\n231\t\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n232\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n233\t\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n234\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n235\t\t\t}\n236\t\t} else {\n237\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\t// Add message\n241\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n242\t\n243\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n244\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n245\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n246\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n247\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n248\t\t\t}\n249\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n250\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n251\t\t\t}\n252\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n253\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n254\t\t\t}\n255\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n256\t\t}\n257\t\n258\t\treturn sb.String()\n259\t}\n260\t\n261\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n262\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n263\t\treturn pe.Err\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n267\t//\n268\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n269\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n270\t//\n271\t// Parameters:\n272\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n273\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n274\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n275\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n276\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n277\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n278\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n279\t//\n280\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n281\t//\n282\t// Example usage:\n283\t//\n284\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n285\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n286\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n287\t\terrorCode := code\n288\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n289\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n290\t\t}\n291\t\n292\t\treturn &ParseError{\n293\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n294\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n295\t\t\tLine:      line,\n296\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n297\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n298\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n299\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n300\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n301\t\t}\n302\t}\n303\t\n304\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n305\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n306\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n307\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n311\t//\n312\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n313\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n314\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n315\ttype ValidationError struct {\n316\t\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n317\t\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n318\t\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n319\t\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n320\t\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n321\t\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n322\t\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n323\t\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n324\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n325\t\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n329\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n330\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n331\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n332\t\t}\n333\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n337\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n338\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n339\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n340\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n341\t\t}\n342\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n343\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n344\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n345\t\t}\n346\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n347\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n348\t\t}\n349\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n350\t}\n351\t\n352\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n353\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n354\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n355\t}\n356\t\n357\t// Error implements the error interface.\n358\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n359\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n360\t\n361\t\t// Build base error with location\n362\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n363\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n364\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n365\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n366\t\t\t}\n367\t\t} else {\n368\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\t// Add field path if available\n372\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n373\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n374\t\t}\n375\t\n376\t\t// Add message\n377\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n378\t\n379\t\t// Add constraint if available\n380\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n381\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\treturn sb.String()\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n388\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n389\t\treturn ve.Err\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n393\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n394\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n395\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n396\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n397\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n398\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n399\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n400\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n401\t\t\t}\n402\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n405\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n406\t\t}\n407\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n408\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n409\t\t}\n410\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n411\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n412\t\t}\n413\t\treturn sb.String()\n414\t}\n415\t\n416\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n417\t//\n418\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n419\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, and error code.\n420\t//\n421\t// Parameters:\n422\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n423\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n424\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n425\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n426\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n427\t//\n428\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n429\t//\n430\t// Example usage:\n431\t//\n432\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\n433\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode) *ValidationError {\n434\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n435\t\terrorCode := code\n436\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n437\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n441\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n442\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n443\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n444\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n445\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n446\t\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n447\t\t}\n448\t}\n449\t\n450\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n451\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n452\t\tvar ve initialization.\n457\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n458\t\treturn &FileError{\n459\t\t\tPath:       path,\n460\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n461\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n462\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n463\t\t}\n464\t}\n465\t\n466\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper actualType,\n487\t\t\tValue:        value,\n488\t\t\tLine:         line,\n489\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n490\t\t}\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\n494\tfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *FieldNotFoundError {\n495\t\treturn creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\n504\tfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, constraintType string, constraint string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *ConstraintError {\n505\t\treturn &ConstraintError{\n506\t\t\tFilePath:       filePath,\n507\t\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n508\t\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n509\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n510\t\t\tValue:          value,\n511\t\t\tLine:           line,\n512\t\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n513\t\t}\n514\t}\n515\t\n516\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n517\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n518\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n519\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n520\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n521\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n522\t\t\t\treturn true\n523\t\t\t}\n524\t\t}\n525\t\treturn false\n526\t}\n527\t\n528\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n529\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n530\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n531\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n532\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n533\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n534\t\t\t\treturn true\n535\t\t\t}\n536\t\t}\n537\t\treturn false\n538\t}\n539\t\n540\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n541\t//\n542\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n543\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n544\ttype FileError struct {\n545\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n546\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n547\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n548\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n549\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n550\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n551\t}\n552\t\n553\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n554\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n555\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n556\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n557\t\t}\n558\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n559\t}\n560\t\n561\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n562\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n563\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n564\t}\n565\t\n566\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n567\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n568\t\top := fe.Operation\n569\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n570\t\t\top = fe.Op\n571\t\t}\n572\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n573\t}\n574\t\n575\t// Error implements the error interface.\n576\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n577\t\top := fe.Operation\n578\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n579\t\t\top = fe.Op\n580\t\t}\n581\t\n582\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n583\t\tif op != \"\" {\n584\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n585\t\t} else {\n586\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n587\t\t}\n588\t\n589\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n590\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n591\t\t}\n592\t\n593\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n594\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n595\t\t}\n596\t\n597\t\treturn sb.String()\n598\t}\n599\t\n600\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n601\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n602\t\treturn fe.Err\n603\t}\n604\t\n605\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n606\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n607\t\tvar fe *FileError\n608\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n609\t}\n610\t\n611\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n612\t//\n613\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n614\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n615\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n616\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n617\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n618\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n619\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n620\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n621\t\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n622\t\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n623\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n627\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n628\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n629\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n630\t\t}\n631\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n632\t}\n633\t\n634\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n635\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n636\t\treturn false\n1054\t}\n1055\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"fdf61cc2-0468-44e5-852a-7deeb9950065","uuid":"8b7fa14a-921f-43b5-ad40-40053639a199","timestamp":"2026-07-11T19:23:36.501Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/errors.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, and error code.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue)\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode) *ValidationError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tType:       ErrorTypeValidation,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n\treturn &FileError{\n\t\tPath:       path,\n\t\tOperation:  operation,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tExpected:   expected,\n\t\tFound:      found,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath string, fieldPath string, expectedType string, actualType string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *TypeMismatchError {\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *FieldNotFoundError {\n\treturn &FieldNotFoundError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath: fieldPath,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, constraintType string, constraint string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *ConstraintError {\n\treturn &ConstraintError{\n\t\tFilePath:       filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\tValue:          value,\n\t\tLine:           line,\n\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Err != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\nfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n\tvar fe *FileError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n}\n\n// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n//\n// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\ntype SyntaxError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn se.Err\n}\n\n// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n//\n// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\ntype StructureError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where structure error occurred\n\tMessage      string    // Description of the structure error\n\tDuplicateKey string    // Name of duplicate key (if applicable)\n\tLocation     string    // Nested path to the error location\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Context() string {\n\tctx := \"location: \" + ste.Location\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", duplicate key: %s\", ste.DuplicateKey)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Line, ste.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ste.Err\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n//\n// TypeMismatchError occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n// expected Go type (e.g., trying to parse a string as an integer).\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with type error\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the field with error\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type description\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found\n\tValue        string    // Actual value that caused the error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif tme.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn tme.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, expected type: %s, actual type: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\ttme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType, tme.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s at line %d, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.Line, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n}\n\n// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n//\n// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n// in the YAML data structure.\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file missing the field\n\tFieldPath string    // Dot-notation path to the missing field\n\tLine      int       // Line number where field should be (if known)\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fnfe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fnfe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeRequiredField\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field not found: %s\", fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Error() string {\n\tif fnfe.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s at line %d: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.Line, fnfe.FieldPath)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// ConstraintError represents constraint validation failures.\n//\n// ConstraintError is used when a field value violates a defined constraint\n// (e.g., value out of range, string too long, pattern mismatch).\ntype ConstraintError struct {\n\tFilePath       string    // Path to the file with constraint error\n\tFieldPath      string    // Dot-notation path to the field\n\tConstraintType string    // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string    // Description of the constraint\n\tValue          string    // Actual value that violated the constraint\n\tLine           int       // Line number where constraint violation occurred\n\tErrorCode      ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ce.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ce.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeConstraintViolation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, constraint: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\tce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint, ce.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Error() string {\n\tif ce.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s at line %d, field %s: %s\",\n\t\t\tce.FilePath, ce.Line, ce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s, field %s: %s\",\n\t\tce.FilePath, ce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint)\n}\n\n// DuplicateKeyError represents an error when duplicate keys are found in YAML.\n//\n// DuplicateKeyError occurs when a YAML mapping contains the same key multiple times,\n// which is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error.\ntype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n\tLine1     int       // Line number of first occurrence\n\tLine2     int       // Line number of duplicate occurrence\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif dke.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dke.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key: %s\", dke.Key)\n\tif dke.Location != code) → *ConstraintError` |\n| `SchemaValidationError` | `NewSchemaValidationError()` | `(filePath, schemaPath, fieldPath, message, expected, found, line, code) → *SchemaValidationError` |\n\n### ❌ Missing Constructor Functions (4 types)\n\n| Error Type | Line Range | Notes |\n|------------|------------|-------|\n| `SyntaxError` | 615-660 | Has `Err` field for underlying yaml parser error |\n| `StructureError` | 665-712 | Has `DuplicateKey` and `Location` fields |\n| `DuplicateKeyError` | 840-881 | Has `Line1`, `Line2` for both occurrences |\n| `SchemaLoadError` | 886-920 | Has `Err` field for underlying error |\n\n---\n\n## Constructor Pattern Analysis\n\n### Constructor Functions Present\n\nAll constructor functions follow this pattern:\n```go\nfunc NewXError(params...) *XError {\n    return &XError{\n        Field1: param1,\n        Field2: param2,\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Example from `NewFileError()`:**\n```go\nfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n    return &FileError{\n        Path:       path,\n        Operation:  operation,\n        Message:    message,\n        ErrorCode:  errorCode,\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### Error Types Without Constructors\n\nThese error types can still be created via direct struct initialization:\n\n```go\n// SyntaxError - example direct construction\nse := &SyntaxError{\n    FilePath:  \"config.yaml\",\n    Line:      10,\n    Column:    5,\n    Message:   \"invalid indentation\",\n    Expected:  \"  \",\n    Found:     \"   \",\n    Err:       underlyingErr,\n    ErrorCode: ErrCodeInvalidSyntax,\n}\n\n// StructureError - example direct construction\nste := &StructureError{\n    FilePath:     \"config.yaml\",\n    Line:         15,\n    Message:      \"duplicate key\",\n    DuplicateKey: \"port\",\n    Location:     \"server\",\n    Err:          underlyingErr,\n    ErrorCode:    ErrCodeDuplicateKey,\n}\n\n// DuplicateKeyError - example direct construction\ndke := &DuplicateKeyError{\n    FilePath:  \"config.yaml\",\n    Key:       \"port\",\n    Location:  \"server\",\n    Line1:     10,\n    Line2:     15,\n    ErrorCode: ErrCodeDuplicateKey,\n}\n\n// SchemaLoadError - example direct construction\nsle := &SchemaLoadError{\n    FilePath:  \"schema.yaml\",\n    Message:   \"invalid schema syntax\",\n    Err:       underlyingErr,\n    ErrorCode: ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed,\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## Test Constructor Requirements\n\nBased on the audit of `internal/yamlutil/errors_test.go`, here's which tests use constructors vs direct construction:\n\n### Tests Using Constructor Functions\n\n| Test | Constructor Used |\n|------|-----------------|\n| `TestNewFileError` | `NewFileError()` |\n| `NewFieldNotFoundError()` |\n| `TestNewConstraintError` | `NewConstraintError()` |\n| `TestNewSchemaValidationError` | `NewSchemaValidationError()` |\n\n### Tests Using Direct Construction (or could `SchemaLoadError` methods | `SchemaLoadError` | Direct struct initialization |\n\n---\n\n## Observations\n\n1. **Error Hierarchy**: The file documents a clear error hierarchy in comments (lines 3-17), showing which errors inherit from which base types.\n\n2. **YAMLError Interface**: All error types implement the `YAMLError` interface with methods:\n   - `Code() ErrorCode`\n   - `YAMLErrorType() ErrorType`\n   - `Context() string`\n\n3. **Consistent Constructor Pattern**: All existing constructors follow the same naming convention (`New{Type}Error()`) and initialization pattern.\n\n4. **Optional vs Required Fields**: Error types without constructors tend to have more optional fields or specialized fields (e.g., `DuplicateKeyError` has `Line1` and `Line2` for tracking both occurrences).\n\n5. **Test Coverage**: Tests that need constructor functions already have them. The missing constructors are for error types that are typically created through direct struct initialization in tests.\n\n---\n\n## Conclusion\n\n**Current state is functional and consistent.** The 7 error types with constructors cover the most common use cases. The 4 error types without constructors (`SyntaxError`, `StructureError`, `DuplicateKeyError`, `SchemaLoadError`) can be created via direct struct initialization and have adequate test coverage using that pattern.\n\n**No urgent action required** - the existing constructors cover the error types most commonly used throughout the codebase.
